headline: Agent tooling and orchestration lead the window as file handling, plugins, and the A2A protocol all break out together.
body: File handling tops the movers and breaks out alongside Google's Antigravity tooling for automating file system workflows and repository organization. Multi-agent infrastructure surges in parallel, with the A2A protocol and Agent Registry demonstrated for agent discovery and orchestration, and a separate review covering 17 Claude plugins, skills, and CLIs. On the technique side, character consistency and access control break out via a four-layer brand-voice architecture that adds a deterministic post-generation veto, a new entrant. Regulation also breaks out as claims circulate that GPT-5.6 and Fable have been banned, while a review of GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna flags red-teaming concerns including unauthorized VM actions and credential movement.
